blockers.description: Hit the weekly Claude usage limit mid-dispatch of the 08-03 gsd-executor Agent() call ('You've hit your weekly limit · resets 5am (Asia/Jakarta)'). No work was lost — the call returned with 0 subagent_tokens and git log confirms zero 08-03 commits exist.
blockers.workaround: Wait for quota reset, then resume by dispatching 08-03's executor normally. Do not retry in a tight loop if the limit message recurs immediately.

decisions.decision: LOGIC-02 race-safety: optimistic locking via prisma.keputusan.updateMany({ where: { id, status: existing.status } }), not a unique constraint or $transaction with row locking
decisions.rationale: Unique constraint doesn't model the real contested resource (decision row status, not city); $transaction+row-locking is disproportionate complexity for this school-project scale; updateMany WHERE-guard is the standard minimal-complexity optimistic-concurrency idiom

decisions.decision: Plan-checker revision round 1: extended 08-06 to wire catatAktivitas into kota/stok mutations (tambahKota/updateKota/hapusKota/setStokTbs), added 08-02 to its depends_on, fixed 08-05's depends_on to include 08-04, moved 08-06 to wave 4 depends_on 08-05
decisions.rationale: First checker pass found 3 blockers: LOGIC-03 silently omitted kota/stok activity logging despite 08-06's threat model claiming coverage; 08-05 imported keputusanService without declaring the dependency; 08-05/08-06 both edited server/src/index.js in the same wave with no ordering

decisions.decision: Used Docker Desktop for local PostgreSQL (switera-db-1) since no native install exists
All executors run sequentially on the main working tree, never isolation=worktree
commit_docs:false discipline strictly enforced through Phase 8 so far — zero .planning/ commits, only server/ code commits per executed task

next_action: Confirm the weekly Claude usage limit has reset, then spawn gsd-executor for 08-03-PLAN.md (permintaan CRUD routes, wave 2, sequential, no worktree, commit_docs:false standing constraint). After it returns, independently re-run its verify script(s) before moving to 08-04.
context_notes: This is an autonomous multi-phase run (v2.0 Phases 6-10) following a 7-rule user instruction (skip-research, chain plan+execute, minimal interaction, commit code only per-phase, one final .planning/ batch commit after Phase 10). Phases 6-7 fully done. Phase 8 planning fully done (6 plans, 1 revision round, checker PASSED). Phase 8 execution: 08-01 and 08-02 both done and independently re-verified live (not just trusting executor self-report) — 08-02's executor actually finished all its work (2 commits + real SUMMARY.md) but its final narration was cut off by the weekly usage limit, which could be mistaken for failed/incomplete work if git log/SUMMARY.md aren't checked first. The next executor dispatch (08-03) hit the same limit before doing any work at all (0 tokens, 0 commits) — that one genuinely needs to be re-run from scratch on resume.
